prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response: The lighthouse keeper, an elderly man named Elias, had seen many a stormy night. But tonight, the fog was thicker than usual, blanketing the shore like a ghostly shroud. As he made his rounds, his weathered boots crunching on the gravel path, a strange sound caught his ear. A soft, rhythmic thumping, almost like a heartbeat. Elias followed the sound, his curiosity piqued, until he reached the base of the lighthouse. There, in the swirling mist, he saw a massive shape emerge. A whale, beached and struggling, its tail thrashing weakly. Elias' heart ached for the creature. He knew he couldn't save it alone. Quickly, he climbed the lighthouse stairs, lit the signal fire, and sent out a distress call. Help would come, but would it be in time? Elias watched the whale, praying to the sea gods for a miracle. In that moment, he realized the true power of his lighthouse - not just to guide ships, but to be a beacon of hope, even for the most unexpected of visitors.
